source: PDF source pdftotext extraction confirms 'carryover' as one word (line 159 of source shows 'capital loss carryover')
evidence: Appears in ¶6 in sentence 'Renee Anderson's proposed findings and proposed distribution of property did not include any reference to the capital loss carryover.' The single-word form 'carryover' is the correct legal and tax terminology, and is used correctly in all other 12+ instances throughout the opinion.

old_exact: 10 years. to the fact subject This Court
source: PDF pdftotext extraction shows page break at that location with no 'to the fact subject' in the source text; this phrase is OCR/ingestion artifact
evidence: Source line 216-218: '...spousal support for 10 years.\n\n             This Court reviews...' vs DB line 73 which inserts 'to the fact subject' between these.

old_exact: the Honorable Daniel E. Gast, Judge.
new_exact: the Honorable Daniel E. Gast, Judicial Referee.
evidence: DB ingestion incorrectly converted 'Judicial Referee' to 'Judge'. The official PDF clearly shows 'Judicial Referee' which is the correct title in the caption. This is a critical factual discrepancy affecting the court's official record.

source: /tmp/src_2023ND39.pdf via pdftotext - pages 5-6, section heading 'III' appears before '[¶22] The appeal is dismissed.'
note: The PDF source shows the Roman numeral section heading 'III' positioned between the end of paragraph [¶21] and the start of paragraph [¶22]. The DB currently lacks this heading in its correct structural location.

evidence: PDF source at line 40-41 shows single 'for plaintiff and appellee' vs. DB line 48 'for for plaintiff and appellee'. Confirmed via byte-exact count and context matching.
note: Duplicate word 'for' is an OCR/ingestion artifact. The authoritative PDF clearly shows only one 'for' in this attorney listing.

old_exact: and which were caused by the issue an compensable impairment award for impairment findings
new_exact: and which were caused by the compensable injury. The organization may not issue an impairment award for impairment findings
evidence: Critical OCR corruption within statute quote (¶14, N.D.C.C. § 65-05-12.2(3) quotation). Phrase 'issue an compensable' is garbled; correct source has 'compensable injury. The organization may not issue an'. Restores proper statutory language and sentence structure.

source: /tmp/src_2023ND16.txt (PDF: 'The agreement does not appear to be substantively')
evidence: The PDF shows 'The agreement does not appear to be substantively unconscionable' at the start of a quoted block. The DB incorrectly has 'to be The agreement does not appear substantively' - the spurious 'to be ' before 'The agreement' is an ingestion artifact, and 'substantively' lacks 'unconscionable' (part of 'to be' phrase bleeding). The PDF context confirms the correct text.
note: Appears in ¶9 within a block quote from district court opinion. The phrase 'to be' is extraneous and breaks the sentence structure.

source: PDF pdftotext extraction confirms 'GMR' at line 172 of authoritative source
note: The defendant company is GMR Transportation (not GMD). This is a case citation (2015 ND 121) appearing at paragraph 10 of Section III. The error is a single-character OCR misread: D→R in the company name. Verified against PDF text which clearly shows 'GMR Transp'.
